content.text:
I have all the information needed. Here's the result:

- The 7 builtin modules under `orbit-tools/src/builtin/` each define `pub fn register(registry: &mut ToolRegistry)` and call `registry.register(...)` within it.
- `orbit-core/src/runtime/builder.rs` creates a `ToolRegistry` and calls `registry.register(ExternalTool {...})` directly.
- `orbit-tools/src/builtin/mod.rs` only delegates to sub-module `register()` functions — no direct `.register()` call on the registry.
- `orbit-engine/src/executor/registry.rs` calls `.register()` on `ActivityExecutorRegistry`, not `ToolRegistry`.
- `orbit-agent/src/runtime/backend.rs` calls `.register()` on an agent-factory registry (no `ToolRegistry` in scope).
- `orbit-tools/src/registry.rs` only *defines* `ToolRegistry::register`.

result:
I have all the information needed. Here's the result:

- The 7 builtin modules under `orbit-tools/src/builtin/` each define `pub fn register(registry: &mut ToolRegistry)` and call `registry.register(...)` within it.
- `orbit-core/src/runtime/builder.rs` creates a `ToolRegistry` and calls `registry.register(ExternalTool {...})` directly.
- `orbit-tools/src/builtin/mod.rs` only delegates to sub-module `register()` functions — no direct `.register()` call on the registry.
- `orbit-engine/src/executor/registry.rs` calls `.register()` on `ActivityExecutorRegistry`, not `ToolRegistry`.
- `orbit-agent/src/runtime/backend.rs` calls `.register()` on an agent-factory registry (no `ToolRegistry` in scope).
- `orbit-tools/src/registry.rs` only *defines* `ToolRegistry::register`.
